commands.description: Load a previous session or start a new one

commands.name: /context
commands.description: Manage context files or show token usage

commands.name: /effort
commands.description: Set thinking effort for this session

commands.name: /feedback
commands.description: Submit feedback, request features, or report issues

commands.name: /goal
commands.description: Set a goal with validation criteria for iterative completion

commands.name: /guide
commands.description: Get help with Kiro CLI features from the guide agent

commands.name: /plan
commands.description: Switch to Plan agent for breaking down ideas into implementation plans

commands.name: /reply
commands.description: Open editor pre-filled with the last assistant message to compose a reply

commands.name: /rewind
commands.description: Rewind conversation to a previous turn (forks into a new session)

commands.name: /stats
commands.description: Show request IDs and timings for debugging slow turns

prompts.name: claude-mem-curate
prompts.description: 把 claude-mem 抽出的 shortlist 精選成少量繁體中文、去重後寫進 AIMemory。當使用者說「精選/整理 claude-mem」「把最近重點存記憶」,或每日自動 ingestion 時使用。

prompts.name: dual-skill-review-loop
prompts.description: Use when producing any deliverable that needs quality assurance — writing, reports, proposals, code designs — and you want the output to reach ≥95 score through structured self-review iterations

prompts.name: excel-to-ai-doc
prompts.description: Convert image-heavy Excel game spec spreadsheets (.xlsx) into an AI-readable knowledge structure — Markdown text + extracted original images (anchored to their source cells) + metadata.json. Use when the user wants to turn a 規格書/spec/design xlsx into something an AI or agent can read, asks to "convert this spec to markdown", "make this xlsx AI-readable", "extract images and tables from this spreadsheet for analysis", or needs to feed a slot/game spec workbook to a vision model. Triggers on game design specs, paytables, UI/flow screenshots embedded in .xlsx. NOT for clean tabular data exports (use a plain csv/markdown converter) or non-xlsx files.

prompts.name: huashu-slides
prompts.description: AI演示文稿全流程制作：内容结构化→设计选型→AI插画/HTML构建→PPTX导出。17种实战验证的视觉风格（漫画/极简/数据叙事/国风），可编辑HTML与全AI视觉两条路径自由选择。当用户提到"做PPT"、"制作幻灯片"、"演示文稿"、"Keynote"、"slides"、"课件"、"汇报材料"、"路演"时使用。

prompts.name: knowhow-accumulation
prompts.description: Use when capturing lessons from AI failures into structured ❌→✅ records, when reviewing past interactions to extract reusable patterns, or when building a self-improving knowledge base from repeated mistakes

prompts.name: memory-to-skill
prompts.description: 當使用者要求「檢視 session 資料夾並把內容整理成 skill」、需要從過去對話中抽取可重用模式、或要把剛剛完成的工作經驗固化成 skill 時使用

prompts.name: ms-agent-scheduled-prompts
prompts.description: 當需要為 LLM agent bridge（Telegram bot、Slack bot、CLI UI）做延遲 / 常態性 prompt 觸發、排程 bridge 指令（如每天凌晨 /restart）、或要在 agent 回覆裡嵌 token 讓使用者自助建排程時使用

prompts.name: ms-agent-text-token-signaling
prompts.description: 當 LLM agent 需要觸發 host 端動作（傳檔、開網址、執行指令）但協定不允許 agent 端發起自訂 RPC、或想在 streaming 文字回覆中加上 side effect 又不破壞聊天 UX 時使用

prompts.name: ms-kiro-strreplace-silent-fail
prompts.description: 當用 Kiro 的 strReplace 或 fs-edit 類工具改檔、tool 回報 Successfully replaced N occurrences 但事後 grep / read 發現磁碟內容沒變、或同一個檔案連續發幾個 strReplace 只有部分生效時使用

prompts.name: ms-portable-skill-authoring
prompts.description: 當寫 skill 會被多個 agent CLI（Kiro / Codex / Claude）或多台機器共用、要避免把 F:\AI\AIMemory 或 ~/.kiro 這類絕對路徑寫死在 SKILL.md、或要讓 bridge 把環境差異注入成變數讓同一份 SKILL.md 跨機器重用時使用

prompts.name: ms-streaming-token-pipeline
prompts.description: Use when a Telegram/Slack bot streams text via mid-turn edits plus a final render. Both paths must share one transform, or ASK buttons drop, half-tokens leak, and rate-limit recovery overwrites it.

prompts.name: ms-telegram-ask-button-protocol
prompts.description: Use when emitting <<ASK:...>> for Telegram bridge buttons, or when an ASK token renders verbatim. Label soft cap 20 chars; >20 SHALL use numbered body list with short labels. See SKILL.md for limits.

prompts.name: ms-telegram-bot-rate-limit-survival
prompts.description: 當 Telegram bot 對使用者 chat 串 streaming edit 或連發多則訊息、遇到 429 Too Many Requests / retry_after 愈累積愈長（60s→183s）、grammY autoRetry 超過 maxDelaySeconds 拋錯、或需要設計「限流期間凍結 UI + 限流結束後自動補發」時使用

prompts.name: ms-wiki-knowledge-base
prompts.description: 當 agent bridge 要維運雙層記憶（facts + wiki 知識庫）時使用：執行 wikisync（topic shard 蒸餾成 wiki 頁）、factlint（facts 去重/去瑣碎/衰減/升格偵測）、wikilint（wiki 過時/孤兒/斷連健康檢查），或要為新 bridge 設計同款 wiki 維運流程時使用

prompts.name: ms-windows-shell-exit-code-false-positive
prompts.description: 當在 Windows PowerShell 或 cmd.exe 中跑 git clone、pip install、npm install、winget 等命令時，明明工作完成卻收到 exit code 1、stderr 有進度條或 notice、shell tool 回報失敗時使用

prompts.name: ms-windows-shell-interactive-prompt
prompts.description: 當在 Windows PowerShell 或 cmd.exe 中執行讀檔、列目錄、搜尋指令（Get-Content、type、cat、more）導致 tool call 卡住不回、或在 Kiro agent 與 ACP bridge 裡看到 PowerShell 的互動式參數詢問（例如 Path 參數提示）時使用

prompts.name: non-engineer-agent-design
prompts.description: Use when designing Custom GPT / Claude Project / Gemini Gem instructions for non-engineers, or when helping someone structure their AI workflow using the 5-piece framework (Agent, Skill, Steering, Hook, KnowHow)

prompts.name: self-eval-prompt-pattern
prompts.description: Use when designing prompts or instructions that require AI to self-assess quality before delivering output, when setting up automated quality gates in Custom GPT / Project / Gem instructions, or when AI outputs need measurable pass/fail criteria

prompts.name: skill-creator
prompts.description: Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.

prompts.name: sync-fork-from-upstream
prompts.description: 當使用者要把自己的 fork / 下游倉庫同步到原專案（upstream）的最新更新時使用。觸發語包含「同步原專案」「拿上游更新」「sync upstream」「更新原專案」「原本的倉庫有更新怎麼拉」。涵蓋一次性 upstream remote 設定、半自動同步流程（fetch → 拋棄式分支試合併 → 型別檢查 → 停在衝突/失敗、絕不自動 push）、以及 fork 與 upstream 無共同歷史時的「重新接血脈」做法。

prompts.name: uk-slot-extrabet
prompts.description: UK 老虎機 ExtraBet（額外押注/加倍投注）子系統指南。建立、修改、除錯 ExtraBet 功能，或處理 unshow/replay 還原、單倍率單步驟 / 多倍率兩步驟跳窗變體、多倍率 type 時觸發。涵蓋 framework ExtraBetComponent / extraBetController、專案 ExtraBetWindow 跳窗、ExtraBetEffectManager 動畫、SlotReels 假轉輪整合、GameView 整合多方關係。

prompts.name: uk-slot-fake-reel-manager
prompts.description: FakeReelManager 假轉輪帶建立、修改、檢查或擴充

prompts.name: uk-slot-spec-to-impl
prompts.description: 當拿到一份 UK 老虎機遊戲規格書（xlsx）要開發新遊戲、使用者說「從規格書實作」「開新的 slot 專案」「規格書轉開發規格」、或要為新遊戲起專案/拆任務時使用。涵蓋 xlsx 轉換、差異開發規格、milestone 任務拆解三步驟。

prompts.name: uk-slot-state-machine
prompts.description: UK 老虎機狀態機設定，新增、移除或修改 SetStateMachine() 遊戲狀態時觸發。

prompts.name: vc-kiro-delegate
prompts.description: 將實作任務委派給 Kiro CLI（headless mode + claude-opus-4.6）執行，把主 agent 的 Claude Max 5x 配額（5 小時 message cap）保留給協調與決策，把實作丟給 Kiro 的不限量訂閱算力。Claude Code 負責預估、規劃、light review；Kiro 負責具體實作與第一輪 self-review。當用戶說「用 kiro 做」「交給 kiro」「省 token」「叫 kiro 寫」時務必使用此 skill；當主 agent 自己評估某個任務「預估會花 2k+ tokens」時也要主動委派 — 例如新增函式、寫測試、重構函式、實作模組。即使用戶沒明說「kiro」，只要預估產出規模 ≥2k tokens，就主動委派並在訊息中告知預估值。

prompts.name: writing-skills
prompts.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment

tools.name: code
tools.description:
Code intelligence with AST parsing and fuzzy search. Language auto-detected from file extension.

CORE FEATURES:
• Fuzzy search for symbols (classes, functions, methods)
• Extracts function/class signatures via AST
• Structural AST search and rewrite (ast-grep)
• Codebase overview and directory exploration

NOTE: LSP operations (find_references, goto_definition, get_hover, get_completions, get_diagnostics, rename_symbol) require LSP initialization.

## Available Operations
- search_symbols: Find symbol definitions by name
- lookup_symbols: Batch lookup specific symbols
- get_document_symbols: List all symbols in a file
- pattern_search: AST-based structural search
- pattern_rewrite: AST-based code transformation
- generate_codebase_overview: High-level codebase structure
- search_codebase_map: Focused directory exploration

tools.name: glob
tools.description:
Find files and directories whose paths match a glob pattern. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Finding files by name pattern (e.g., "*.rs", "**/*.tsx")
- Discovering project structure
- Listing files in specific directories

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a glob pattern to match files
- Optionally specify a root directory to search from
- Optionally specify a limit on results and max depth

PATTERNS:
- "*.rs" - All .rs files in current directory
- "**/*.rs" - All .rs files recursively
- "src/**/*.{ts,tsx}" - All TypeScript files under src/

tools.name: goal
tools.description:
Signal goal completion. A goal is a binding completion contract — the system will continue re-prompting you until the contract is satisfied.

Commands:
- complete: Certify that ALL success criteria defined in the goal have been met, verified by concrete evidence you produced during this session (test output, file contents, command results). Your summary must cite the specific evidence that satisfies each criterion. A completion without cited verification will not satisfy the contract.

COMPLETION STANDARD (treat as binding):
1. Each criterion in the goal MUST be individually verified by tool output you can cite.
2. Belief, assumption, or narrative confidence is NOT evidence. Only tool results count.
3. If ANY criterion lacks verifiable evidence, you MUST NOT call complete. Continue working.
4. If a verification path is blocked, try alternative approaches before giving up. Only after exhausting alternatives should you end your turn without calling complete — the system will re-prompt you on the next iteration with a chance to try differently.
5. If across multiple iterations you identify a genuinely impossible impediment (same hard blocker confirmed repeatedly despite different approaches), you MAY call complete with a summary that documents: what was accomplished, what is blocked, the evidence of the blocker, and what would unblock it. This is the ONLY path to early completion without full success — and requires cited evidence of the blocker itself.

STRATEGY:
- Divide complex goals into independent sub-tasks. Use sub-agents to parallelize work when the goal has separable components (e.g., implement + test, frontend + backend).
- When available, delegate verification to a sub-agent acting as an independent auditor. An external reviewer that inspects the work product without sharing your context produces stronger evidence of completion than self-assessment.
- Prefer divide-and-conquer: decompose, delegate, verify independently, then synthesize.

tools.name: grep
tools.description:
Fast text pattern search in files using regex. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Searching for literal text patterns, error messages, TODOs, config values
- Finding files containing specific text

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- For semantic code understanding, use the code tool instead
- For finding symbol definitions or usages, use the code tool

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a regex pattern to search for
- Optionally specify a path to search from (defaults to current directory)
- Optionally specify a file filter glob (e.g., "*.rs", "*.{ts,tsx}")

OUTPUT MODES:
- content: Show matching lines with file path and line number (default)
- files_with_matches: Only show file paths that contain matches
- count: Show count of matches per file

tools.name: introspect
tools.description:
Look up documentation about this chat application's own features, slash commands, settings, or capabilities.

WHEN TO USE:
- User asks about this assistant's features, commands, or settings
- User wants to know what slash commands are available
- User asks how to use a specific feature of this chat application

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- General coding questions, AWS help, or tasks the user wants you to perform
- Questions unrelated to this chat application itself

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a query to search the documentation
- Or provide a doc_path to retrieve a specific document
- When mentioning commands in your response, always prefix them with '/' (e.g., '/chat save', '/chat load', '/context')
- CRITICAL: Only provide information explicitly documented. If details are not documented, clearly state the information is not available rather than generating assumptions.

tools.name: knowledge
tools.description:
A tool for indexing and searching content across chat sessions using semantic search.

## Overview
This tool enables persistent storage and retrieval of information using semantic search (MiniLLM) or keyword search (BM25). Content remains available across sessions for later use.

## When to use
- When users ask to query your knowledge bases or kbs
- When you need to search previously indexed content
- When users request to index new content (code, markdown, CSV, PDF, and other text file formats)
- When exploring unfamiliar content to find relevant information
- When users ask about topics that might be in indexed knowledge bases

## When not to use
- When content has not been indexed yet and user hasn't requested indexing
- When you need real-time or external information not in the knowledge base

## Notes
- Use 'show' command to list available knowledge bases before searching
- Search can target specific knowledge bases (context_id) or all knowledge bases
- Use default limit values unless specifically needed; fewer results for focused search
- Pagination available via offset parameter for large result sets
- 'add' command indexes new content; 'update' command refreshes existing knowledge bases
- Unless there is a clear reason to modify the search query, use the user's original wording for better semantic matching

tools.name: read
tools.description:
Tool for reading files, directories and images. Always provide an 'operations' array.

For single operation: provide array with one element.
For batch operations: provide array with multiple elements.

Available modes:
- Line: Read lines from a file
- Directory: List directory contents
- Image: Read and process images

Examples:
1. Single: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file.txt"}]}
2. Batch: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file1.txt"}, {"mode": "Directory", "path": "/src"}]}
3. Image: {"operations": [{"mode": "Image", "image_paths": ["/path/to/image.png"]}]}

tools.name: shell
tools.description:
A tool for executing shell commands.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use only as a last-resort when no other available tool can accomplish the task

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the command to execute

FEATURES:

LIMITATIONS:
- Executes commands using the detected shell on Windows (PowerShell or cmd.exe)

TIPS:
- Use the fileRead and fileWrite tools for reading and modifying files

tools.name: subagent
tools.description:
Spawn and coordinate multiple AI agents in a pipeline (DAG). Each stage runs as a
persistent session. Stages with no depends_on start immediately in parallel.

MODES:
- background (not yet implemented): Fire-and-forget. Returns immediately, results arrive in inbox.
- blocking (default): Waits for all stages to complete, returns consolidated results.

USE THIS when you need multi-step work with specialized agents:
- Research → Implement → Review pipelines
- Parallel research tracks that feed into a single implementer
- Any workflow where stages have dependencies

LOOPS:
- Add loop_to on a stage to create iterative cycles (e.g., reviewer loops back to implementer)
- trigger: text in the stage's output that triggers the loop (e.g., "NEEDS_CHANGES")
- max_iterations: safety cap to prevent infinite loops
- The target stage re-runs with the triggering stage's feedback as context

Each stage becomes a session you can monitor via ctrl+g in the TUI.

tools.name: todo_list
tools.description: A tool for creating a task list and keeping track of tasks. This tool should be requested EVERY time the user gives you a task that will take multiple steps. A task list should be made BEFORE executing any steps. Steps should be marked off AS YOU COMPLETE THEM. DO NOT display your own tasks or task list AT ANY POINT; this is done for you. Complete the tasks in the same order that you provide them. If the user tells you to skip a step, DO NOT mark it as completed.

tools.name: use_aws
tools.description: Make an AWS CLI api call with the specified service, operation, and parameters. All arguments MUST conform to the AWS CLI specification. Should the output of the invocation indicate a malformed command, invoke help to obtain the the correct command.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch and extract content from a specific URL. Supports three modes: 'selective' (default, extracts relevant sections around search terms), 'truncated' (first 8000 chars), 'full' (complete content).

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: WebSearch looks up information that is outside the model's training data or cannot be reliably inferred from the current codebase/context.

tools.name: write
tools.description:
A tool for creating and editing text files.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use when you need to create a new file, or modify an existing file
- Perfect for updating text-based file formats

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the path to the file you want to create or modify
- Specify the operation to perform: one of `create`, `strReplace`, or `insert`
- Use `create` to create a new file. Required parameter is `content`. Parent directories will be created if they are missing.
- Use `strReplace` to replace and update the content of an existing file.
- Use `insert` to insert content at a specific line, or append content to the end of a file.

TIPS:
- To append content to the end of a file, use `insert` with no `insert_line`

tools.name: google_docs_append
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Append plain text to the end of a Google Doc.

tools.name: google_docs_create
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Create a Google Doc, optionally with initial text.

tools.name: google_docs_get
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Read a Google Doc's title and plain text.

tools.name: google_docs_replace_text
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Replace all matching plain text in a Google Doc.

tools.name: google_drive_search_docs
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Search Google Drive for Google Docs by title.

tools.name: google_maps_geocode
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Convert an address or place description to coordinates.

tools.name: google_maps_route
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Compute a route between two addresses or named places.

tools.name: google_maps_search_places
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Search Google Places by natural-language query.

tools.name: code
tools.description:
Code intelligence with AST parsing and fuzzy search. Language auto-detected from file extension.

CORE FEATURES:
• Fuzzy search for symbols (classes, functions, methods)
• Extracts function/class signatures via AST
• Structural AST search and rewrite (ast-grep)
• Codebase overview and directory exploration

NOTE: LSP operations (find_references, goto_definition, get_hover, get_completions, get_diagnostics, rename_symbol) require LSP initialization.

## Available Operations
- search_symbols: Find symbol definitions by name
- lookup_symbols: Batch lookup specific symbols
- get_document_symbols: List all symbols in a file
- pattern_search: AST-based structural search
- pattern_rewrite: AST-based code transformation
- generate_codebase_overview: High-level codebase structure
- search_codebase_map: Focused directory exploration

tools.name: glob
tools.description:
Find files and directories whose paths match a glob pattern. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Finding files by name pattern (e.g., "*.rs", "**/*.tsx")
- Discovering project structure
- Listing files in specific directories

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a glob pattern to match files
- Optionally specify a root directory to search from
- Optionally specify a limit on results and max depth

PATTERNS:
- "*.rs" - All .rs files in current directory
- "**/*.rs" - All .rs files recursively
- "src/**/*.{ts,tsx}" - All TypeScript files under src/

tools.name: goal
tools.description:
Signal goal completion. A goal is a binding completion contract — the system will continue re-prompting you until the contract is satisfied.

Commands:
- complete: Certify that ALL success criteria defined in the goal have been met, verified by concrete evidence you produced during this session (test output, file contents, command results). Your summary must cite the specific evidence that satisfies each criterion. A completion without cited verification will not satisfy the contract.

COMPLETION STANDARD (treat as binding):
1. Each criterion in the goal MUST be individually verified by tool output you can cite.
2. Belief, assumption, or narrative confidence is NOT evidence. Only tool results count.
3. If ANY criterion lacks verifiable evidence, you MUST NOT call complete. Continue working.
4. If a verification path is blocked, try alternative approaches before giving up. Only after exhausting alternatives should you end your turn without calling complete — the system will re-prompt you on the next iteration with a chance to try differently.
5. If across multiple iterations you identify a genuinely impossible impediment (same hard blocker confirmed repeatedly despite different approaches), you MAY call complete with a summary that documents: what was accomplished, what is blocked, the evidence of the blocker, and what would unblock it. This is the ONLY path to early completion without full success — and requires cited evidence of the blocker itself.

STRATEGY:
- Divide complex goals into independent sub-tasks. Use sub-agents to parallelize work when the goal has separable components (e.g., implement + test, frontend + backend).
- When available, delegate verification to a sub-agent acting as an independent auditor. An external reviewer that inspects the work product without sharing your context produces stronger evidence of completion than self-assessment.
- Prefer divide-and-conquer: decompose, delegate, verify independently, then synthesize.

tools.name: grep
tools.description:
Fast text pattern search in files using regex. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Searching for literal text patterns, error messages, TODOs, config values
- Finding files containing specific text

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- For semantic code understanding, use the code tool instead
- For finding symbol definitions or usages, use the code tool

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a regex pattern to search for
- Optionally specify a path to search from (defaults to current directory)
- Optionally specify a file filter glob (e.g., "*.rs", "*.{ts,tsx}")

OUTPUT MODES:
- content: Show matching lines with file path and line number (default)
- files_with_matches: Only show file paths that contain matches
- count: Show count of matches per file

tools.name: introspect
tools.description:
Look up documentation about this chat application's own features, slash commands, settings, or capabilities.

WHEN TO USE:
- User asks about this assistant's features, commands, or settings
- User wants to know what slash commands are available
- User asks how to use a specific feature of this chat application

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- General coding questions, AWS help, or tasks the user wants you to perform
- Questions unrelated to this chat application itself

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a query to search the documentation
- Or provide a doc_path to retrieve a specific document
- When mentioning commands in your response, always prefix them with '/' (e.g., '/chat save', '/chat load', '/context')
- CRITICAL: Only provide information explicitly documented. If details are not documented, clearly state the information is not available rather than generating assumptions.

tools.name: knowledge
tools.description:
A tool for indexing and searching content across chat sessions using semantic search.

## Overview
This tool enables persistent storage and retrieval of information using semantic search (MiniLLM) or keyword search (BM25). Content remains available across sessions for later use.

## When to use
- When users ask to query your knowledge bases or kbs
- When you need to search previously indexed content
- When users request to index new content (code, markdown, CSV, PDF, and other text file formats)
- When exploring unfamiliar content to find relevant information
- When users ask about topics that might be in indexed knowledge bases

## When not to use
- When content has not been indexed yet and user hasn't requested indexing
- When you need real-time or external information not in the knowledge base

## Notes
- Use 'show' command to list available knowledge bases before searching
- Search can target specific knowledge bases (context_id) or all knowledge bases
- Use default limit values unless specifically needed; fewer results for focused search
- Pagination available via offset parameter for large result sets
- 'add' command indexes new content; 'update' command refreshes existing knowledge bases
- Unless there is a clear reason to modify the search query, use the user's original wording for better semantic matching

tools.name: read
tools.description:
Tool for reading files, directories and images. Always provide an 'operations' array.

For single operation: provide array with one element.
For batch operations: provide array with multiple elements.

Available modes:
- Line: Read lines from a file
- Directory: List directory contents
- Image: Read and process images

Examples:
1. Single: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file.txt"}]}
2. Batch: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file1.txt"}, {"mode": "Directory", "path": "/src"}]}
3. Image: {"operations": [{"mode": "Image", "image_paths": ["/path/to/image.png"]}]}

tools.name: shell
tools.description:
A tool for executing shell commands.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use only as a last-resort when no other available tool can accomplish the task

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the command to execute

FEATURES:

LIMITATIONS:
- Executes commands using the detected shell on Windows (PowerShell or cmd.exe)

TIPS:
- Use the fileRead and fileWrite tools for reading and modifying files

tools.name: subagent
tools.description:
Spawn and coordinate multiple AI agents in a pipeline (DAG). Each stage runs as a
persistent session. Stages with no depends_on start immediately in parallel.

MODES:
- background (not yet implemented): Fire-and-forget. Returns immediately, results arrive in inbox.
- blocking (default): Waits for all stages to complete, returns consolidated results.

USE THIS when you need multi-step work with specialized agents:
- Research → Implement → Review pipelines
- Parallel research tracks that feed into a single implementer
- Any workflow where stages have dependencies

LOOPS:
- Add loop_to on a stage to create iterative cycles (e.g., reviewer loops back to implementer)
- trigger: text in the stage's output that triggers the loop (e.g., "NEEDS_CHANGES")
- max_iterations: safety cap to prevent infinite loops
- The target stage re-runs with the triggering stage's feedback as context

Each stage becomes a session you can monitor via ctrl+g in the TUI.

tools.name: todo_list
tools.description: A tool for creating a task list and keeping track of tasks. This tool should be requested EVERY time the user gives you a task that will take multiple steps. A task list should be made BEFORE executing any steps. Steps should be marked off AS YOU COMPLETE THEM. DO NOT display your own tasks or task list AT ANY POINT; this is done for you. Complete the tasks in the same order that you provide them. If the user tells you to skip a step, DO NOT mark it as completed.

tools.name: use_aws
tools.description: Make an AWS CLI api call with the specified service, operation, and parameters. All arguments MUST conform to the AWS CLI specification. Should the output of the invocation indicate a malformed command, invoke help to obtain the the correct command.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch and extract content from a specific URL. Supports three modes: 'selective' (default, extracts relevant sections around search terms), 'truncated' (first 8000 chars), 'full' (complete content).

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: WebSearch looks up information that is outside the model's training data or cannot be reliably inferred from the current codebase/context.

tools.name: write
tools.description:
A tool for creating and editing text files.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use when you need to create a new file, or modify an existing file
- Perfect for updating text-based file formats

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the path to the file you want to create or modify
- Specify the operation to perform: one of `create`, `strReplace`, or `insert`
- Use `create` to create a new file. Required parameter is `content`. Parent directories will be created if they are missing.
- Use `strReplace` to replace and update the content of an existing file.
- Use `insert` to insert content at a specific line, or append content to the end of a file.

TIPS:
- To append content to the end of a file, use `insert` with no `insert_line`

NOTE: LSP operations (find_references, goto_definition, get_hover, get_completions, get_diagnostics, rename_symbol) require LSP initialization.

## Available Operations
- search_symbols: Find symbol definitions by name
- lookup_symbols: Batch lookup specific symbols
- get_document_symbols: List all symbols in a file
- pattern_search: AST-based structural search
- pattern_rewrite: AST-based code transformation
- generate_codebase_overview: High-level codebase structure
- search_codebase_map: Focused directory exploration

tools.name: glob
tools.description:
Find files and directories whose paths match a glob pattern. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Finding files by name pattern (e.g., "*.rs", "**/*.tsx")
- Discovering project structure
- Listing files in specific directories

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a glob pattern to match files
- Optionally specify a root directory to search from
- Optionally specify a limit on results and max depth

PATTERNS:
- "*.rs" - All .rs files in current directory
- "**/*.rs" - All .rs files recursively
- "src/**/*.{ts,tsx}" - All TypeScript files under src/

tools.name: goal
tools.description:
Signal goal completion. A goal is a binding completion contract — the system will continue re-prompting you until the contract is satisfied.

Commands:
- complete: Certify that ALL success criteria defined in the goal have been met, verified by concrete evidence you produced during this session (test output, file contents, command results). Your summary must cite the specific evidence that satisfies each criterion. A completion without cited verification will not satisfy the contract.

COMPLETION STANDARD (treat as binding):
1. Each criterion in the goal MUST be individually verified by tool output you can cite.
2. Belief, assumption, or narrative confidence is NOT evidence. Only tool results count.
3. If ANY criterion lacks verifiable evidence, you MUST NOT call complete. Continue working.
4. If a verification path is blocked, try alternative approaches before giving up. Only after exhausting alternatives should you end your turn without calling complete — the system will re-prompt you on the next iteration with a chance to try differently.
5. If across multiple iterations you identify a genuinely impossible impediment (same hard blocker confirmed repeatedly despite different approaches), you MAY call complete with a summary that documents: what was accomplished, what is blocked, the evidence of the blocker, and what would unblock it. This is the ONLY path to early completion without full success — and requires cited evidence of the blocker itself.

STRATEGY:
- Divide complex goals into independent sub-tasks. Use sub-agents to parallelize work when the goal has separable components (e.g., implement + test, frontend + backend).
- When available, delegate verification to a sub-agent acting as an independent auditor. An external reviewer that inspects the work product without sharing your context produces stronger evidence of completion than self-assessment.
- Prefer divide-and-conquer: decompose, delegate, verify independently, then synthesize.

tools.name: grep
tools.description:
Fast text pattern search in files using regex. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Searching for literal text patterns, error messages, TODOs, config values
- Finding files containing specific text

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- For semantic code understanding, use the code tool instead
- For finding symbol definitions or usages, use the code tool

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a regex pattern to search for
- Optionally specify a path to search from (defaults to current directory)
- Optionally specify a file filter glob (e.g., "*.rs", "*.{ts,tsx}")

OUTPUT MODES:
- content: Show matching lines with file path and line number (default)
- files_with_matches: Only show file paths that contain matches
- count: Show count of matches per file

tools.name: introspect
tools.description:
Look up documentation about this chat application's own features, slash commands, settings, or capabilities.

WHEN TO USE:
- User asks about this assistant's features, commands, or settings
- User wants to know what slash commands are available
- User asks how to use a specific feature of this chat application

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- General coding questions, AWS help, or tasks the user wants you to perform
- Questions unrelated to this chat application itself

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a query to search the documentation
- Or provide a doc_path to retrieve a specific document
- When mentioning commands in your response, always prefix them with '/' (e.g., '/chat save', '/chat load', '/context')
- CRITICAL: Only provide information explicitly documented. If details are not documented, clearly state the information is not available rather than generating assumptions.

tools.name: knowledge
tools.description:
A tool for indexing and searching content across chat sessions using semantic search.

## Overview
This tool enables persistent storage and retrieval of information using semantic search (MiniLLM) or keyword search (BM25). Content remains available across sessions for later use.

## When to use
- When users ask to query your knowledge bases or kbs
- When you need to search previously indexed content
- When users request to index new content (code, markdown, CSV, PDF, and other text file formats)
- When exploring unfamiliar content to find relevant information
- When users ask about topics that might be in indexed knowledge bases

## When not to use
- When content has not been indexed yet and user hasn't requested indexing
- When you need real-time or external information not in the knowledge base

## Notes
- Use 'show' command to list available knowledge bases before searching
- Search can target specific knowledge bases (context_id) or all knowledge bases
- Use default limit values unless specifically needed; fewer results for focused search
- Pagination available via offset parameter for large result sets
- 'add' command indexes new content; 'update' command refreshes existing knowledge bases
- Unless there is a clear reason to modify the search query, use the user's original wording for better semantic matching

tools.name: read
tools.description:
Tool for reading files, directories and images. Always provide an 'operations' array.

For single operation: provide array with one element.
For batch operations: provide array with multiple elements.

Available modes:
- Line: Read lines from a file
- Directory: List directory contents
- Image: Read and process images

Examples:
1. Single: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file.txt"}]}
2. Batch: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file1.txt"}, {"mode": "Directory", "path": "/src"}]}
3. Image: {"operations": [{"mode": "Image", "image_paths": ["/path/to/image.png"]}]}

tools.name: shell
tools.description:
A tool for executing shell commands.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use only as a last-resort when no other available tool can accomplish the task

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the command to execute

FEATURES:

LIMITATIONS:
- Executes commands using the detected shell on Windows (PowerShell or cmd.exe)

TIPS:
- Use the fileRead and fileWrite tools for reading and modifying files

tools.name: subagent
tools.description:
Spawn and coordinate multiple AI agents in a pipeline (DAG). Each stage runs as a
persistent session. Stages with no depends_on start immediately in parallel.

MODES:
- background (not yet implemented): Fire-and-forget. Returns immediately, results arrive in inbox.
- blocking (default): Waits for all stages to complete, returns consolidated results.

USE THIS when you need multi-step work with specialized agents:
- Research → Implement → Review pipelines
- Parallel research tracks that feed into a single implementer
- Any workflow where stages have dependencies

LOOPS:
- Add loop_to on a stage to create iterative cycles (e.g., reviewer loops back to implementer)
- trigger: text in the stage's output that triggers the loop (e.g., "NEEDS_CHANGES")
- max_iterations: safety cap to prevent infinite loops
- The target stage re-runs with the triggering stage's feedback as context

Each stage becomes a session you can monitor via ctrl+g in the TUI.

tools.name: todo_list
tools.description: A tool for creating a task list and keeping track of tasks. This tool should be requested EVERY time the user gives you a task that will take multiple steps. A task list should be made BEFORE executing any steps. Steps should be marked off AS YOU COMPLETE THEM. DO NOT display your own tasks or task list AT ANY POINT; this is done for you. Complete the tasks in the same order that you provide them. If the user tells you to skip a step, DO NOT mark it as completed.

tools.name: use_aws
tools.description: Make an AWS CLI api call with the specified service, operation, and parameters. All arguments MUST conform to the AWS CLI specification. Should the output of the invocation indicate a malformed command, invoke help to obtain the the correct command.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch and extract content from a specific URL. Supports three modes: 'selective' (default, extracts relevant sections around search terms), 'truncated' (first 8000 chars), 'full' (complete content).

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: WebSearch looks up information that is outside the model's training data or cannot be reliably inferred from the current codebase/context.

tools.name: write
tools.description:
A tool for creating and editing text files.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use when you need to create a new file, or modify an existing file
- Perfect for updating text-based file formats

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the path to the file you want to create or modify
- Specify the operation to perform: one of `create`, `strReplace`, or `insert`
- Use `create` to create a new file. Required parameter is `content`. Parent directories will be created if they are missing.
- Use `strReplace` to replace and update the content of an existing file.
- Use `insert` to insert content at a specific line, or append content to the end of a file.

TIPS:
- To append content to the end of a file, use `insert` with no `insert_line`

prompt.text:
[Telegram bridge tool: send file]
`<<SEND_FILE:絕對路徑>>` — 附檔給使用者（圖片≤10MB走photo，餘走document）。路徑須在 TG_SEND_ROOTS 底下。
僅在使用者明確要求傳檔時加；產生/儲存檔案不構成傳檔意圖，回報路徑即可。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: ask with buttons]
`<<ASK:id|key=label|*key=label|...>>`（單行）— 渲染 inline keyboard，點擊回送 `[ASK:id] key`。
限制：id≤32 key≤20 [a-zA-Z0-9_-]；label≤40 不含`|``=`；1-8選項；`*`標推薦（至多一個）。
選項說明長時正文列 1./2./3.，label 只放短標題。違反限制會原封顯示無法渲染。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: schedule]
`<<SCHEDULE:<time>|<prompt>>>`（單行）— 建立延遲/常態性排程。
一次性：`30s`/`15m`/`2h`/`1d`/`HH:MM`/`tomorrow HH:MM`/`MM-DD HH:MM`/`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`。
常態性：`every 30m`/`daily 09:00`/`weekly mon 09:00`/`cron */5 * * * *`。
prompt≤1000字，不可含`>>`。單則至多3個。僅在使用者明確要求排程時使用。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: report skill use]
`<<SKILL_USED:skill-name|reason>>`（單行）— 你參考了某 SKILL.md 時加。
name=資料夾名 kebab-case≤60；reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。一則至多5個，重複去重。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: goal done signal]
`<<GOAL_DONE:reason>>`（單行）— /goal 標的達成時加在回覆最後。
reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。未完成不加。/goal 未啟動時忽略。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: restart]
`<<RESTART:reason>>`（單行）— 重啟 session（context 過大、工具異常、需刷新 preamble）。
重要：restart 前先 remember() 存工作狀態，格式：
  - `[WS] task: <目標>` — 當前高層任務
  - `[WS] completed: <已完成項>` — 本 session 完成的事（可多條）
  - `[WS] blocked: <阻塞項>` — 等待中的事
  - `[WS] key_refs: <重要路徑或值>` — 需帶到下個 session 的參考
  - `[WS] next_action: <下一步>` — 恢復後第一件要做的事
增量存檔：多步驟任務每完成一個里程碑就先 remember() 一條 `[WS] completed:`，
不要等 restart 才存——session 可能非計畫性中斷（crash / idle / force-kill）。
Guardrail：同 /goal 最多5次，間隔≥5分鐘。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: sticker]
`<<STICKER:emotion>>`（單行）— 發送 video sticker。
可用：loading, cry, love, hello, refuse, sad, angry, sleep, happy, shock, agree, clap, ok, sorry, thanks, good。適時使用（打招呼/完成/道歉等），不要每則都加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: wiki query]
`<<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>>`（單行）— 標記此回覆值得存為 wiki page。
slug=[a-z0-9_-]≤80；summary≤200字描述主題。Bridge 會自動整理成 wiki/queries/ 頁面。
條件：回覆≥200字 + 跨3+不同主題的綜合性回答。短回覆/操作確認不加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: propose skill]
`<<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>`（單行）— 提議把本次對話模式寫成 skill。
name=kebab-case≤60（將成為 ms-{name}/ 資料夾名）；reason≤500 說明為何值得。
Bridge 發確認按鈕，使用者同意才萃取。僅在發現可跨專案重用的模式時加，一則至多1個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: continue]
`<<CONTINUE>>`（單行）— 告訴 bridge 你還沒做完，需要再一輪。
Bridge 收到後自動排程下一個 turn，不需使用者介入。
使用時機：單次回覆做不完（tool call 太多、要改多個檔案、多步驟任務）。
限制：/goal 啟動時不需要用（goal 系統自帶 continuation）；一則只認一個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: specialist proxy]
`<<SPECIALIST_PROXY:name|使用者的原始訊息>>`（單行）— 把使用者的訊息轉交給本機 specialist 即時回答。
進入 proxy 模式後，使用者後續訊息直送 specialist，回覆直送使用者，直到使用者說 /back。
Specialist 的記憶會回流到你的 facts。proxy 結束時你會收到對話摘要。

可用 specialist：
- slot-dev（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/slot-dev）
- researcher（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/researcher）
- general（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/general）

使用時機：使用者的問題明確屬於某 specialist 的領域，且需要即時互動（不是背景任務）。
背景任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；即時對話用 SPECIALIST_PROXY。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: parallel delegate]
`<<PARALLEL_DELEGATE:task_id|specialist_name|goal prompt>>`（單行）— 並行委派多個任務給 specialist。
同一個 turn 放多個 PARALLEL_DELEGATE，bridge 會同時 spawn 多個獨立 instance 並行執行。
全部完成後結果一次注入你的 session（格式：[PARALLEL_RESULT:task_id]）。

格式規則：
- task_id：唯一識別碼（如 `pt_1`、`pt_2`）
- specialist_name：目標 specialist（同一個 name 可以開多個 instance）
- goal prompt：任務描述（不可含 `>>` 或 `|`），同樣應含三要素：目標與動機 / 驗收條件 / 回報格式
- 每個 task 獨立 instance，互不干擾——但要避免多個 task 改同一個檔案
- ⚠️ 同樣需要至少一個 tool call 防止 CLI 吞 token

使用時機：任務可拆成多個獨立子任務且各自耗時 > 1 分鐘（如同時研究多支影片、同時改不同模組）。
單一任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；可並行的多任務用 PARALLEL_DELEGATE。
[End tool]

[Environment — machine-specific paths the bridge resolved for this session]
Skills and notes that need filesystem paths should reference these variables,
not hard-coded values. This lets the same SKILL.md work on multiple machines.
- MEMORY_DIR=G:\AI\AIMemory
- SKILL_DIR=C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.kiro\skills
- AGENT_CONFIG_DIR=C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.kiro
- USER_ID=509424983
Conventions: sessions are under ${MEMORY_DIR}/sessions/, archived under
${MEMORY_DIR}/oldSessions/, master fact log at ${MEMORY_DIR}/facts-${USER_ID}.md,
topic shards at ${MEMORY_DIR}/facts_Topic/${USER_ID}/<topic>.md.
Agent-wide config files (agent configs) live under
${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/.
Specialist 歷史產出存在 ${MEMORY_DIR}/artifacts/（結構化 JSON），需要時用 read tool 查閱。
[End environment]

[Project context — AI.md layered loading]
工作目錄可能有 `AI.md` 檔案記錄該項目/目錄的上下文要點。
規則：讀到或修改某目錄的檔案時，檢查該目錄及上層（最多 3 層）有無 AI.md。
有就用 read tool 讀取作為工作上下文。同 session 同一個 AI.md 只需讀一次。
多層都有時全部讀（根先子後）。AI.md ≤2000 字，寫架構/慣例/踩坑/測試指引。
主動寫入：在某目錄新增≥3檔案、做架構性改動、或踩坑修復時，主動建立/更新 AI.md。
SPEC：任務≥3工項且跨≥2檔案時先開 SPEC.md；完成工項標 [x]；session 結束前更新進度。
預設行為是寫，不是不寫。詳見 POLICIES/proactive-write-policy.md。
CodeGraph：首次對某專案做修改或追呼叫鏈時，用 codegraph_status(projectPath) 檢查該專案有無索引。
有索引→優先用 codegraph_context/trace/impact 而非手動 grep；改函式簽名前必須先 codegraph_impact。
無索引→回報使用者，建議建立。已知有索引的專案以 codegraph_status 回傳為準。
[End project context]

[Agent disciplines]
MEMORY — store: preferences, decisions, architecture/env choices, reusable lessons, tool configs, naming rules.
Skip: one-time confirmations, transient status, session-only facts, anything in code/commits or re-derivable in <30s.
Test: useless in a new session 2 weeks later → don't store. Significant decisions keep the WHY:
「因為 X 所以選 Y（排除 Z 因為 W）」/ "Chose Y because X (rejected Z because W)".
REFLEXION — same approach fails ≥2 times (tool error, no-match edit, failing build) → STOP, diagnose in 1-2
sentences, switch to a fundamentally different approach (different tool/strategy, or ask user). Never retry a 3rd time.
READ-BACK — after every file write/edit, verify the changed region with read/grep before reporting done.
Never trust the tool's success status alone (strReplace-style silent fails happen).
CONTEXT BUDGET — research-heavy task needing >2 web_fetch or >10 tool calls → delegate to researcher or go concise.
Prefer snippets/summaries over full fetches. On ⚠️ CONTEXT BUDGET WARNING: stop research, produce output now.
Reports: gather conclusions first (≤3 searches), then write the file in ONE call.
ASK BUTTONS — presenting 2+ alternatives, yes/no confirmation, or 「要不要做 X？」→ MUST append <<ASK:...>>.
Self-check triggers: 「哪個」「要選」「方案A/B」「which」「prefer」. Options listed without ASK → add the token before finishing.
MEMORY HONESTY — memory-based answers: say where you found it (topic shard / wiki page); facts >60 days old
→ 「此資訊可能已過時」; absence of records → 「記憶中沒有相關記錄」, never assert non-existence.
Applies only to brain questions (people/decisions/history/preferences), not code or general knowledge.
CAPABILITY GAPS — clumsy pattern recurs ≥2 times → write+test a script in scripts/, then confirm via <<ASK:...>>
before adopting (declined → delete); broader patterns → <<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>. Check [Available scripts]
first. Never silently add tools; never propose for one-offs.

# Telegram’s Bot API Now Lets Autonomous AI Agents Coordinate Directly — With No Federal Multi-Agent Security Standard in Place

#### On May 7, the platform enabled native bot-to-bot communication for the first time, opening Telegram’s billion-user infrastructure to multi-agent AI workflows that researchers say outpace current security frameworks

By [ Adrian Parham ][12]
Published: May 18 2026, 12:36 PM EDT
[Share on Facebook][13] [Share on Twitter][14] [Share on LinkedIn][15] [Share on Reddit][16] [Share on Flipboard][17] [Share on Pocket][18]
[Chatbot Creation in Telegram]
Telegram. org

On May 7, 2026, Telegram became the first billion-user messaging platform to enable native, direct communication between autonomous AI bots — a capability that developers and AI researchers have sought for years and one that lands well ahead of any enacted federal standard for securing the multi-agent systems it makes possible. The [update][19], announced by founder and CEO Pavel Durov in a post that also introduced more than ten other new features, expands the Telegram Bot API to allow what the company calls "bot-to-bot communication mode. " Under the new system, one bot can send a private message directly to another bot by referencing its @username — but only when both the sender and the recipient have explicitly opted into the mode. That mutual opt-in is a deliberate design choice intended to prevent automated spam chains. The change removes a technical barrier that had existed since the Bot API launched more than a decade ago. Until May 7, Telegram bots could not address each other directly; developers who needed multi-agent workflows had to route messages through a custom server acting as an intermediary. That workaround is now unnecessary. ### Telegram's Bot API Gains a Native Multi-Agent Coordination Layer

Telegram outlined several concrete use cases in its announcement: a code-review bot receiving a request from a collaborator bot and returning results to a human-monitored group chat; enterprise booking and customer-service bots delegating sub-tasks to specialist bots; and multi-step AI workflows that execute end-to-end without any human relay point. In each scenario, users who choose to watch can observe bot-to-bot conversations, making Telegram's infrastructure function as an observable coordination layer rather than a black-box backend. The practical significance is that Telegram's Bot API already hosts [more than 10 million bots][20]. Those bots can now form networks. A developer deploying an AI research agent, for example, can have that agent offload a data-retrieval subtask to a dedicated specialist bot and receive the result back — all within Telegram's native message delivery infrastructure, using an audience of more than one billion existing users as a deployment environment. The same update introduced Chat Automation, which lets individual Telegram users connect a bot to their own profile to handle replies on their behalf, with user-configurable controls over which conversations the bot can access. Bots also now support Streaming Text, displaying responses progressively as a model generates them. A Custom AI Styles feature lets users write prompts that define writing voices, shareable via links. ### Late March's Managed Bots Feature Set the Stage; Developers Responded the Same Day

The bot-to-bot announcement builds directly on Managed Bots, introduced in the [March 31, 2026][21] update. That feature lets a single "manager bot" create and configure personalized agent bots for individual users in two taps, eliminating the manual BotFather token setup. The combination — Managed Bots for frictionless deployment, bot-to-bot communication for agent coordination — constitutes a coherent agentic platform within a consumer messaging app. Developer response was immediate. On May 7, the team behind OpenClaw, an open-source AI agent runtime that uses Telegram as its primary communication layer, filed a [GitHub issue][22] the same day requesting implementation support for the new feature. The issue describes a four-agent setup — agents named Ari, Finn, Remi, and Tai — in which one agent would delegate a billing query to a specialist peer and surface the answer to a human, with no human chat thread serving as an intermediary relay. Before May 7, the team had built a workaround routing system because Telegram bots could not see each other; the new API removes that constraint at the platform level. ### Multi-Agent Security Research Identifies Risks the Platform Does Not Yet Address

The same week Telegram shipped the feature, the security community was actively publishing on the risks of exactly these kinds of systems. A [March 2026 study][23] by researchers at Georgia Institute of Technology, Kennesaw State University, and the OWASP Foundation surveyed seventeen existing security frameworks against multi-agent system cybersecurity risks and found that the best-covered framework — the OWASP Agentic Security Initiative — addressed only 65. 3 percent of the identified threat categories. Non-determinism and data leakage were identified as the two most under-addressed risk domains across all frameworks reviewed. A separate [2025 paper][24] from researchers at the Cooperative AI Foundation identified three structural failure modes specific to multi-agent architectures — miscoordination, conflict, and collusion — and seven underlying risk factors including network effects, emergent agency, and inter-agent trust exploitation, in which a breach in one agent cascades through the trust relationships between others in a network. Oxford researcher Christian Schroeder de Witt, writing on open challenges in multi-agent security, identified secret collusion and coordinated swarm attacks as novel threat classes that existing cybersecurity frameworks were not built to detect. None of these risks are unique to Telegram, and the bot-to-bot feature includes the mutual opt-in requirement as a structural safeguard. But no enacted federal law specifically governs multi-agent AI systems running on consumer messaging infrastructure. The OWASP Agentic Security Initiative and NIST's AI Risk Management Framework provide voluntary guidance; neither carries enforcement authority. Telegram's own history adds context without constituting a direct risk from this feature. The platform's Bot API has been documented as a command-and-control channel by malware families including DeerStealer and Lumma Stealer, according to a [December 2025 NVISO research report][25]. Telegram blocked more than 34 million groups and channels in 2025 following increased law enforcement cooperation after Durov's August 2024 arrest in France, where he remains under a formal criminal investigation for alleged complicity in organized crime on the platform. ### Telegram's Broader Positioning: Open Infrastructure for AI Competition

Telegram's trajectory on this feature set is deliberate. In its announcement, the company described itself as "the only platform where all AI models can compete freely — while users have full control over their experience," framing the Bot API as neutral infrastructure for AI services rather than a single-assistant product. That positioning is notable. Where most agentic AI deployment environments remain inside enterprise tooling or developer sandboxes with limited user bases, Telegram is building these capabilities into a platform with more than one billion monthly active users, an open bot-creation model, and a consistent track record of shipping API expansions faster than governance frameworks can follow. The bot-to-bot feature is the clearest expression of that strategy yet: not a product built for AI, but existing communications infrastructure repurposed as a deployment layer for AI coordination networks. For developers, the practical question is no longer whether multi-agent coordination on Telegram is possible. It is. The questions now are how to secure agent trust chains in a platform-native environment, what the mutual opt-in requirement does and does not protect against, and whether the voluntary security frameworks available today are adequate for what is being built. ⓒ 2026 TECHTIMES. com All rights reserved. # Bot API changelog

> The Bot API is an HTTP-based interface created for developers keen on building bots for Telegram.
> To learn how to create and set up a bot, please consult our [**Introduction to Bots »**][8]

You will find all changes to our [**Bot API**][9] on this page.

### Recent changes

> Subscribe to [@BotNews][10] to be the first to know about the latest updates and join the discussion in [@BotTalk][11]

### 2026

#### June 11, 2026

**Bot API 10.1**

**Rich Messages**
* Added support for [Rich Messages][12], allowing bots to send highly structured text and stream AI-generated replies with seamless rich formatting.
* Added the classes [RichTextBold][13], [RichTextItalic][14], [RichTextUnderline][15], [RichTextStrikethrough][16], [RichTextSpoiler][17], [RichTextDateTime][18], [RichTextTextMention][19], [RichTextSubscript][20], [RichTextSuperscript][21], [RichTextMarked][22], [RichTextCode][23], [RichTextCustomEmoji][24], [RichTextMathematicalExpression][25], [RichTextUrl][26], [RichTextEmailAddress][27], [RichTextPhoneNumber][28], [RichTextBankCardNumber][29], [RichTextMention][30], [RichTextHashtag][31], [RichTextCashtag][32], [RichTextBotCommand][33], [RichTextAnchor][34], [RichTextAnchorLink][35], [RichTextReference][36] and [RichTextReferenceLink][37], which represent different types of rich formatted text.
* Added the class [RichText][38], which represents rich formatted text.
* Added the class [RichBlockCaption][39], which represents the caption of a rich formatted text.
* Added the class [RichBlockTableCell][40], which represents a cell in a table.
* Added the class [RichBlockListItem][41], which represents an item in a list.
* Added the classes [RichBlockParagraph][42], [RichBlockSectionHeading][43], [RichBlockPreformatted][44], [RichBlockFooter][45], [RichBlockDivider][46], [RichBlockMathematicalExpression][47], [RichBlockAnchor][48], [RichBlockList][49], [RichBlockBlockQuotation][50], [RichBlockPullQuotation][51], [RichBlockCollage][52], [RichBlockSlideshow][53], [RichBlockTable][54], [RichBlockDetails][55], [RichBlockMap][56], [RichBlockAnimation][57], [RichBlockAudio][58], [RichBlockPhoto][59], [RichBlockVideo][60], [RichBlockVoiceNote][61] and [RichBlockThinking][62], which represent different types of blocks in a rich formatted message.
* Added the class [RichBlock][63], which represents a block in a rich formatted message.
* Added the class [RichMessage][64], which represents a rich formatted message.
* Added the field *rich_message* to the class [Message][65].
* Added the class [InputRichMessage][66], describing a rich message to send.
* Added the class [InputRichMessageContent][67] and allowed it to be used as [InputMessageContent][68] in results of inline, guest, and Web App queries.
* Added the method [sendRichMessage][69], allowing bots to send rich messages.
* Added the method [sendRichMessageDraft][70], allowing bots to stream partial rich messages.
* Added the parameter *rich_message* to the method [editMessageText][71], allowing bots to edit rich messages.

**Join Request Queries**
* Added the field *supports_join_request_queries* to the class [User][72].
* Added the field *guard_bot* to the class [ChatFullInfo][73].
* Added the field *query_id* to the class [ChatJoinRequest][74].
* Added the method [answerChatJoinRequestQuery][75].
* Added the method [sendChatJoinRequestWebApp][76].

**Polls**
* Added the class [Link][77] and the field *link* to the class [PollMedia][78].
* Added the class [InputMediaLink][79] and allowed it to be used as [InputPollOptionMedia][80].

#### May 8, 2026

**Bot API 10.0**

**Guest Mode**
* Introduced support for [guest mode][81], allowing bots to receive certain messages and issue replies within chats they are not a member of.
* Added the field *supports_guest_queries* to the class [User][82].
* Added the fields *guest_bot_caller_user* and *guest_bot_caller_chat* to the class [Message][83].
* Added the field *guest_query_id* to the class [Message][84].
* Added the field *guest_message* to the class [Update][85].
* Added the class [SentGuestMessage][86] and the method [answerGuestQuery][87].

**Chat Management**
* Added the field *can_react_to_messages* to the classes [ChatMemberRestricted][88] and [ChatPermissions][89].
* Added the parameter *return_bots* to the method [getChatAdministrators][90].
* Added the method [deleteAllMessageReactions][91].
* Added the method [deleteMessageReaction][92].
* Added the ability to see certain messages sent by other bots in groups.

**Polls**
* Added the classes [InputMediaSticker][93], [InputMediaLocation][94], and [InputMediaVenue][95].
* Added the class [PollMedia][96], representing a media in a poll.
* Added the field *media* to the class [Poll][97], allowing bots to see media in polls.
* Added the field *explanation_media* to the class [Poll][98], allowing bots to see media in quiz explanations.
* Added the field *media* to the class [PollOption][99], allowing bots to see media in poll options.
* Added the class [InputPollMedia][100] and the parameters *media* and *explanation_media* to the method [sendPoll][101], allowing bots to add media to polls.
* Added the class [InputPollOptionMedia][102] and the field *media* to the class [InputPollOption][103], allowing bots to add media to poll options.
* Added the field *members_only* to the class [Poll][104].
* Added the parameter *members_only* to the method [sendPoll][105].
* Added the field *country_codes* to the class [Poll][106].
* Added the parameter *country_codes* to the method [sendPoll][107].
* Decreased the minimum number of poll options from 2 to 1.

**Live photos**
* Added the class [LivePhoto][108], which represents a photo with a short video.
* Added the class [InputMediaLivePhoto][109].
* Added the field *live_photo* to the classes [Message][110] and [ExternalReplyInfo][111].
* Added the method [sendLivePhoto][112], allowing bots to send live photos.
* Added the class [PaidMediaLivePhoto][113], which describes a paid media with a live photo.
* Added the class [InputPaidMediaLivePhoto][114], allowing bots to send live photos as paid media.
* Allowed to use live photos in [sendMediaGroup][115] and [editMessageMedia][116],

**General**
* Allowed Business Bots to manage user accounts without a Telegram Premium subscription.
* Added the ability to send messages to other bots via username if both bots enabled bot-to-bot communication.
* Added the ability to reply to other bots from a business bot if the business bot enabled bot-to-bot communication.
* Allowed bots to pass an empty text in the method [sendMessageDraft][117].
* Added the class [BotAccessSettings][118] and the method [getManagedBotAccessSettings][119].
* Added the method [setManagedBotAccessSettings][120].
* Added the method [getUserPersonalChatMessages][121].

#### April 3, 2026

**Bot API 9.6**

**Managed Bots**
* Added the field *can_manage_bots* to the class [User][122].
* Added the class [KeyboardButtonRequestManagedBot][123] and the field *request_managed_bot* to the class [KeyboardButton][124].
* Added the class [ManagedBotCreated][125] and the field *managed_bot_created* to the class [Message][126].
* Added updates about the creation of managed bots and the change of their token, represented by the class [ManagedBotUpdated][127] and the field *managed_bot* in the class [Update][128].
* Added the methods [getManagedBotToken][129] and [replaceManagedBotToken][130].
* Added the class [PreparedKeyboardButton][131] and the method [savePreparedKeyboardButton][132], allowing bots to request users, chats and managed bots from Mini Apps.
* Added the method *requestChat* to the class [WebApp][133].
* Added support for `https://t.me/newbot/{manager_bot_username}/{suggested_bot_username}[?name={suggested_bot_name}]` links, allowing bots to request the creation of a managed bot via a link.

**Polls**
* Added support for quizzes wi[Content truncated - showing first 8000 characters]

items.Text:
After a successful call, you can immediately log in on a local server, but will not be able to log in back to the cloud Bot API server for 10 minutes. Returns True on success. Requires no parameters. ^{[Source][136]}

### close [][137]

ts

`close(signal?: [AbortSignal][138]);
`

Use this method to close the bot instance before moving it from one local server to another. You need to delete the webhook before calling this method to ensure that the bot isn’t launched again after server restart. The method will return error 429 in the first 10 minutes after the bot is launched. Returns True on success. Requires no parameters. ^{[Source][139]}

### sendMessage [][140]

ts

`sendMessage(
  chat_id: number | string,
  text: string,
  other?: Other<[R][141], "sendMessage", "chat_id" | "text">,
  signal?: [AbortSignal][142],
);
`

Use this method to send text messages. On success, the sent Message is returned. ^{[Source][143]}

### sendRichMessage [][144]

ts

`sendRichMessage(
  chat_id: number | string,
  rich_message: [InputRichMessage][145],
  other?: Other<[R][146], "sendRichMessage", "chat_id" | "rich_message">,
  signal?: [AbortSignal][147],
);
`

Use this method to send rich messages. If the message contains a block with a media element, then the bot must have the right to send the media to the chat. On success, the sent Message is returned. ^{[Source][148]}

### forwardMessage [][149]

ts

`forwardMessage(
  chat_id: number | string,
  from_chat_id: number | string,
  message_id: number,
  other?: Other<[R][150], "forwardMessage", "chat_id" | "from_chat_id" | "message_id">,
  signal?: [AbortSignal][151],
);
`

Use this method to forward messages of any kind. Service messages and messages with protected content can’t be forwarded. On success, the sent Message is returned. ^{[Source][152]}

### forwardMessages [][153]

ts

`forwardMessages(
  chat_id: number | string,
  from_chat_id: number | string,
  message_ids: number[],
  other?: Other<[R][154], "forwardMessages", "chat_id" | "from_chat_id" | "message_ids">,
  signal?: [AbortSignal][155],
);
`

Use this method to forward multiple messages of any kind. If some of the specified messages can’t be found or forwarded, they are skipped. Service messages and messages with protected content can’t be forwarded. Album grouping is kept for forwarded messages. On success, an array of MessageId of the sent messages is returned. On success, the edited Message is returned. ^{[Source][262]}

### sendDice [][263]

ts

`sendDice(
  chat_id: number | string,
  emoji: (string & Record<never, never>) | "🎲" | "🎯" | "🏀" | "⚽" | "🎳" | "🎰",
  other?: Other<[R][264], "sendDice", "chat_id" | "emoji">,
  signal?: [AbortSignal][265],
);
`

Use this method to send an animated emoji that will display a random value. On success, the sent Message is returned. ^{[Source][266]}

### setMessageReaction [][267]

ts

`setMessageReaction(
  chat_id: number | string,
  message_id: number,
  reaction: [ReactionType][268][],
  other?: Other<[R][269], "setMessageReaction", "chat_id" | "message_id" | "reaction">,
  signal?: [AbortSignal][270],
);
`

Use this method to change the chosen reactions on a message. Service messages of some types can’t be reacted to. Automatically forwarded messages from a channel to its discussion group have the same available reactions as messages in the channel. Bots can’t use paid reactions. Returns True on success. ^{[Source][271]}

### sendMessageDraft [][272]

ts

`sendMessageDraft(
  chat_id: number,
  draft_id: number,
  text: string,
  other?: Other<[R][273], "sendMessageDraft", "chat_id" | "draft_id" | "text">,
  signal?: [AbortSignal][274],
);
`

Use this method to stream a partial message to a user while the message is being generated. Returns True on success. ^{[Source][275]}

### sendRichMessageDraft [][276]

ts

`sendRichMessageDraft(
  chat_id: number,
  draft_id: number,
  rich_message: [InputRichMessage][277],
  other?: Other<[R][278], "sendRichMessageDraft", "chat_id" | "draft_id" | "rich_message">,
  signal?: [AbortSignal][279],
);
`

Use this method to stream a partial rich message to a user while the message is being generated. Note that the streamed draft is ephemeral and acts as a temporary 30-second preview - once the output is finalized, you must call sendRichMessage with the complete message to persist it in the user’s chat. Returns True on success. ^{[Source][280]}

### sendChatAction [][281]

ts

`sendChatAction(
  chat_id: number | string,
  action: "typing" | "upload_photo" | "record_video" | "upload_video" | "record_voice" | "upload_voice" | "upload_document" | "choose_sticker" | "find_location" | "record_video_note" | "upload_video_note",
  other?: Other<[R][282], "sendChatAction", "chat_id" | "action">,
  signal?: [AbortSignal][283],
);
`

Use this method when you need to tell the user that something is happening on the bot’s side. The status is set for 5 seconds or less (when a message arrives from your bot, Telegram clients clear its typing status). Returns True on success. Example: The ImageBot needs some time to process a request and upload the image. Instead of sending a text message along the lines of “Retrieving image, please wait…”, the bot may use sendChatAction with action = upload_photo. The user will see a “sending photo” status for the bot. We only recommend using this method when a response from the bot will take a noticeable amount of time to arrive. ^{[Source][284]}

### getUserProfilePhotos [][285]

ts

`getUserProfilePhotos(
  user_id: number,
  other?: Other<[R][286], "getUserProfilePhotos", "user_id">,
  signal?: [AbortSignal][287],
);
`

Use this method to get a list of profile pictures for a user. Returns a UserProfilePhotos object. ^{[Source][555]}

### getChatMenuButton [][556]

ts

`getChatMenuButton(other?: Other<[R][557], "getChatMenuButton">, signal?: [AbortSignal][558]);
`

Use this method to get the current value of the bot’s menu button in a private chat, or the default menu button. Returns MenuButton on success. ^{[Source][559]}

### setMyDefaultAdministratorRights [][560]

ts

`setMyDefaultAdministratorRights(other?: Other<[R][561], "setMyDefaultAdministratorRights">, signal?: [AbortSignal][562]);
`

Use this method to the change the default administrator rights requested by the bot when it’s added as an administrator to groups or channels. These rights will be suggested to users, but they are are free to modify the list before adding the bot. Returns True on success. ^{[Source][563]}

### getMyDefaultAdministratorRights [][564]

ts

`getMyDefaultAdministratorRights(other?: Other<[R][565], "getMyDefaultAdministratorRights">, signal?: [AbortSignal][566]);
`

Use this method to get the current default administrator rights of the bot. Returns ChatAdministratorRights on success. ^{[Source][567]}

### getMyStarBalance [][568]

ts

`getMyStarBalance(signal?: [AbortSignal][569]);
`

A method to get the current Telegram Stars balance of the bot. Requires no parameters. On success, returns a StarAmount object. ^{[Source][570]}

### editMessageText [][571]

ts

`editMessageText(
  chat_id: number | string,
  message_id: number,
  text_or_rich_message: string | [InputRichMessage][572],
  other?: Other<[R][573], "editMessageText", "chat_id" | "message_id" | "inline_message_id" | "text" | "rich_message">,
  signal?: [AbortSignal][574],
);
`

Use this method to edit text, rich and game messages. On success, if the edited message is not an inline message, the edited Message is returned, otherwise True is returned. Note that business messages that were not sent by the bot and do not contain an inline keyboard can only be edited within 48 hours from the time they were sent. ^{[Source][575]}

### editMessageTextInline [][576]

ts

`editMessageTextInline(
  inline_message_id: string,
  text_or_rich_message: string | [InputRichMessage][577],
  other?: Other<[R][578], "editMessageText", "chat_id" | "message_id" | "inline_message_id" | "text" | "rich_message">,
  signal?: [AbortSignal][579],
);
`

Use this method to edit text, rich and game inline messages. On success, if the edited message is not an inline message, the edited Message is returned, otherwise True is returned. Note that business messages that were not sent by the bot and do not contain an inline keyboard can only be edited within 48 hours from the time they were sent. ^{[Source][580]}

### editMessageCaption [][581]

ts

`editMessageCaption(
  chat_id: number | string,
  message_id: number,
  other?: Other<[R][582], "editMessageCaption", "chat_id" | "message_id" | "inline_message_id">,
  signal?: [AbortSignal][583],
);
`

Use this method to edit captions of messages. On success, if the edited message is not an inline message, the edited Message is returned, otherwise True is returned. Note that business messages that were not sent by the bot and do not contain an inline keyboard can only be edited within 48 hours from the time they were sent. ^{[Source][584]}

### editMessageCaptionInline [][585]

ts

`editMessageCaptionInline(
  inline_message_id: string,
  other?: Other<[R][586], "editMessageCaption", "chat_id" | "message_id" | "inline_message_id">,
  signal?: [AbortSignal][587],
);
`

Use this method to edit captions of inline messages. On success, if the edited message is not an inline message, the edited Message is returned, otherwise True is returned. Note that business messages that were not sent by the bot and do not contain an inline keyboard can only be edited within 48 hours from the time they were sent. ^{[Source][588]}

### editMessageMedia [][589]

ts

`editMessageMedia(
  chat_id: number | string,
  message_id: number,
  media: [InputMedia][590],
  other?: Other<[R][591], "editMessageMedia", "chat_id" | "message_id" | "inline_message_id" | "media">,
  signal?: [AbortSignal][592],
);
`

Use this method to edit animation, audio, document, live photo, photo, or video messages, or to replace a text or a rich message with a media. If a message is part of a message album, then it can be edited only to an audio for audio albums, only to a document for document albums and to a photo, a live photo, or a video otherwise. org/docs/Web/API/AbortSignal

# Streaming Message Drafts (`stream`) [][114]

This plugin lets you stream long text messages to Telegram. Any [iterator][115] of string snippets can be streamed right into any private chat.

For example, you can make LLM output [appear gradually][116] while generating the response.

## Quickstart [][117]

The plugin installs three methods on the [context object][118]:
* [`ctx.replyWithStream`][119]: plain message streaming
* [`ctx.replyWithMarkdownStream`][120]: markdown streaming (**recommended**)
* [`ctx.replyWithHtmlStream`][121]: HTML streaming

Plain text streaming (first option) sends regular text messages. The other two methods use Telegram’s [rich messages][122] and are recommended for most cases.

> Streaming messages performs many API calls very rapidly. It is strongly recommended to use the [auto-retry plugin][123] alongside the stream plugin.

TypeScriptJavaScriptDeno
ts

`import { Bot, type Context } from "grammy";
import { autoRetry } from "@grammyjs/auto-retry";
import { stream, type StreamFlavor } from "@grammyjs/stream";

type MyContext = StreamFlavor<Context>;
const bot = new Bot<MyContext>("");

bot.api.config.use(autoRetry()); // strongly recommended!
bot.use(stream());

async function* slowText() {
  // emulate slow text generation
  yield "This is som";
  await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 2000));
  yield "e slowly gen";
  await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, 2000));
  yield "erated text";
}

// Telegram only supports streaming in private chats.
bot.chatType("private")
  .command("stream", async (ctx) => {
    // Stream the message!
    await ctx.replyWithStream(slowText());
  });

bot.start();`

## Rich Messages 完整解析（Bot API 10.1，2026-06-11）

### 核心概念

Rich Messages 是 Telegram 為 AI bot 量身打造的結構化訊息格式，與舊的 `parse_mode` (Markdown/HTML) 完全不同——它是 **block-based** 架構，類似 Notion / Telegraph 的 block editor。

### 三個關鍵 API 方法

| 方法 | 用途 | 重要特性 |
|------|------|---------|
| `sendRichMessageDraft` | **串流預覽**（AI 生成中） | 暫存 30 秒的 ephemeral draft，同一 `draft_id` 動畫更新 |
| `sendRichMessage` | **最終持久化** | Draft 完成後必須呼叫此方法，訊息才會留在 chat |
| `editMessageText` + `rich_message` 參數 | **編輯** | 可後續修改已發送的 Rich Message |

### 串流工作流程（你的 bridge 最想要的）

```
1. 開始生成 → sendRichMessageDraft(chat_id, draft_id=1, rich_message={markdown: "正在思考..."})
2. 中間 chunk  → sendRichMessageDraft(chat_id, draft_id=1, rich_message={markdown: "# 結果\n部分內容..."})
3. 生成完畢 → sendRichMessage(chat_id, rich_message={markdown: "# 完整結果\n..."})
```

**關鍵點**：
- `draft_id` 相同的 draft 會**動畫過渡**（不閃爍），這就是原生 streaming！
- Draft 只存 **30 秒**，超時消失——所以長時間生成需持續呼叫
- Draft 只限 **private chat**（不支援群組）
- `<tg-thinking>` 標籤只能用在 draft 裡（顯示「思考中」動畫）

### InputRichMessage — 兩種輸入格式

```typescript
interface InputRichMessage {
  html?: string;        // HTML 格式（二選一）
  markdown?: string;    // Rich Markdown 格式（二選一）
  is_rtl?: boolean;
  skip_entity_detection?: boolean;
}
```

**Rich Markdown**（推薦，相容 GitHub Flavored Markdown）：

```markdown
# 標題
## 子標題

**粗體** *斜體* ~~刪除線~~ `程式碼`

```typescript
const x = 1;
```（語言高亮）

> 引用
> > 巢狀引用

| 表頭1 | 表頭2 |
|-------|-------|
| 值1   | 值2   |

![圖片](https://example.com/photo.jpg "圖說")

$$E = mc^2$$（LaTeX 公式區塊）

---（分隔線）
```

**Rich HTML 額外標籤**（Markdown 做不到的）：

```html
<u>底線</u>
<sub>下標</sub>  <sup>上標</sup>
<mark>高亮</mark>
<a name="anchor-1"></a>（錨點）
<aside>Pull quote<cite>作者</cite></aside>
<details open><summary>摺疊標題</summary>內容</details>
<tg-map lat="25.0" long="121.5" zoom="14"/>
<tg-collage>...<figcaption>說明</figcaption></tg-collage>
<tg-slideshow>...</tg-slideshow>
<tg-math-block>E = mc^2</tg-math-block>
<tg-thinking>正在思考...</tg-thinking>  ← 僅 draft
```

### 支援的 RichBlock 類型（21 種）

| 類別 | Block |
|------|-------|
| 文字 | Paragraph, SectionHeading(1-6), Preformatted, Footer |
| 結構 | List(含 checkbox), Table, Details(摺疊), Divider |
| 引用 | BlockQuotation, PullQuotation |
| 媒體 | Photo, Video, Animation, Audio, VoiceNote, Collage, Slideshow, Map |
| 特殊 | MathematicalExpression(LaTeX), Anchor, **Thinking**(僅 draft) |

```
Agent 回覆 chunk
  → session.buffer 累積
  → session.onUpdate() throttle
  → 不做 markdownToHtml()，直接用 raw markdown
  → ctx.api.raw.sendRichMessageDraft({ chat_id, draft_id, rich_message: {markdown} })
  → [每 25 秒 heartbeat 防 30s timeout]

## 結果：grammY 完整支援 Bot API 10.1 Rich Messages

### 原生 API 支援（grammY 1.44 core）

grammY 1.44 的 `Api` class 已經包含所有 Rich Message 方法，type-safe：

```typescript
// 送 Rich Message（最終持久化）
ctx.api.sendRichMessage(chatId, { markdown: "..." }, { reply_markup: ... });

// 串流 Draft（30 秒暫存，動畫更新）
ctx.api.sendRichMessageDraft(chatId, draftId, { markdown: "..." });

// 編輯既有訊息為 Rich（text 或 InputRichMessage 二選一）
ctx.api.editMessageText(chatId, msgId, { markdown: "..." });
// ↑ 注意：editMessageText 的第三參數現在接受 string | InputRichMessage
```

### 官方 Stream Plugin（`@grammyjs/stream` v1.1.0）⭐

grammY 有**官方 streaming plugin**，直接封裝了 draft lifecycle：

```typescript
import { stream, type StreamFlavor } from "@grammyjs/stream";

bot.use(stream());

// 只要給它一個 async iterator，剩下全自動：
await ctx.replyWithMarkdownStream(asyncIterator);
// 內部自動：
//   1. 產生 draft_id
//   2. 每次 yield 呼叫 sendRichMessageDraft（動畫更新）
//   3. 30 秒 timeout 前自動 heartbeat
//   4. iterator 結束時呼叫 sendRichMessage 持久化
```

提供三種模式：
- `ctx.replyWithStream(iter)` — 純文字 streaming（用 sendMessageDraft）
- `ctx.replyWithMarkdownStream(iter)` — **Markdown Rich Message**（推薦）
- `ctx.replyWithHtmlStream(iter)` — HTML Rich Message

| 面向 | 發現 |
|------|------|
| grammY types | ✅ 已包含 `RichMessage`, `InputRichMessage`, `RichBlock`, `sendRichMessageDraft` 全套 type（`grammy_types@v3.28.0`）|
| API 方法 | ✅ `sendRichMessage` + `sendRichMessageDraft` + `editMessageText(rich_message)` 都有 |
| 官方 plugin | ✅ `@grammyjs/stream` v1.1.0 直接做完 draft lifecycle（heartbeat、final persist） |
| 版本需求 | grammY 1.44.0 + `@grammyjs/stream@1.1.0`（你目前已經是 1.44） |

### Bridge 升級路線修正

有了官方 plugin，改動量**大幅縮減**：

| 原本預估 | 有 plugin 之後 |
|---------|---------------|
| 自己實作 draft_id 管理 | ❌ 不需要，plugin 內建 |
| 自己寫 30 秒 heartbeat | ❌ 不需要，plugin 內建 |
| 重寫 `telegram-rich-renderer.ts` | 只需薄薄一層包裝 |
| 改 `run-prompt.ts` 4 處 | 核心只改 1 處：把 buffer 轉成 async iterator 接給 plugin |

**最小改動路線**：

1. `npm install @grammyjs/stream`（1 分鐘）
2. `index.ts` 或 `bot-setup.ts` 加 `bot.use(stream())`（1 行）

### 唯一需要注意的

- Plugin 的 `replyWithMarkdownStream` 假設你給它一個完整的 async iterator（yield string chunks）——但 bridge 的 ACP session 是 push-based（`session.onUpdate` callback），需要一個 adapter 把 push → pull（用 `AsyncGenerator` + resolve/reject pattern）
- 群組不支援 streaming draft——你的 bot 是 1:1 私聊，所以沒問題
